tasks.title: Create output directory
tasks.description: Create the directory target/temp/swarmout if it does not exist. Ensure the directory is writable and clean any existing files that may conflict with the new output.

tasks.title: Review current product
tasks.description: Examine the source code and documentation of the current product (the repository where the request originates). Produce a concise markdown file current_product.md that lists existing major features, architecture components, and any known limitations or open issues. Place the file in target/temp/swarmout.

tasks.title: Analyze ClaudeCode competitor
tasks.description: Read the source code located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Identify key features, user workflows, and any unique capabilities that differentiate it from the current product. Summarize findings in a markdown file claude_code_analysis.md placed in target/temp/swarmout.

tasks.title: Analyze OpenCode competitor
tasks.description: Read the source code located at ../opencode. Document the main functionalities, integration points, and any advanced features (e.g., AI prompting, code generation pipelines). Output the results to opencode_analysis.md in target/temp/swarmout.

tasks.title: Analyze Copilot‑CLI competitor
tasks.description: Research and, if source is available, examine the copilot-cli tool. Capture its primary command‑line features, configuration mechanisms, and extension capabilities. Produce copilot_cli_analysis.md in target/temp/swarmout.

tasks.title: Analyze Roocode competitor
tasks.description: Gather information on roocode (public repositories, documentation, or release notes). Identify the features it offers that are not present in the current product. Summarize the analysis in roocode_analysis.md placed in target/temp/swarmout.

tasks.title: Generate SWARMOUT2.md with feature suggestions and implementation plan
tasks.description:
Combine the outputs from s2‑s6 to create a comprehensive document SWARMOUT2.md. The document should:
1. List suggested feature additions for the current product, referencing gaps identified in each competitor analysis.
2. Provide a prioritized roadmap with detailed Milestones and associated Tasks for implementing each new feature. Each task should include a concise description, estimated effort (e.g., story points), and any required dependencies.
3. Follow a clear markdown structure (e.g., ## Suggested Features, ## Milestones, ### Milestone 1, #### Tasks, etc.).
Place SWARMOUT2.md in target/temp/swarmout.
Ensure the plan is realistic and actionable for a development team.
